title: E2E UI + visual parity (Playwright e2e-browser) — ORIGINAL re-baseline on host SurfaceBookPro9
against: ORIGINAL (pristine TS freshell, dist/server/index.js built from this worktree) run as an EXTERNAL target on 127.0.0.1:17871 with an isolated scratch $HOME; this profile is the comparison target for the Rust port on THIS host

how_produced.config: port/oracle/t3/playwright.target.config.ts (external-target mode; the shared, unmodified spec suite)
how_produced.command: PLAYWRIGHT_JSON_OUTPUT_NAME=<raw> FRESHELL_E2E_TARGET_URL=http://127.0.0.1:17871 FRESHELL_E2E_TARGET_TOKEN=<token> FRESHELL_E2E_TARGET_HOME=<scratch> npx playwright test --config port/oracle/t3/playwright.target.config.ts --reporter=list,json
how_produced.server: PORT=17871 AUTH_TOKEN=<token> FRESHELL_BIND_HOST=127.0.0.1 HOME=<scratch> FRESHELL_HOME=<scratch> NODE_ENV=production node dist/server/index.js — isolated scratch home pre-seeded with {version:1,settings:{network:{configured:true,host:"127.0.0.1"}}} (identical to what the E2E TestServer writes) so the setup wizard is bypassed
how_produced.isolation: Single external ORIGINAL server on 127.0.0.1:17871 with an isolated $HOME under the user home; reaped after the run. No live user server was touched.
how_produced.flake_classification: NOT performed this run — single pass at workers=1 retries=0 per the re-baseline protocol (red is data). See the note for the prior host's hard/flaky classification of the 8 shared failures.

flake_reclassifications.evidence: 4/4 isolated re-runs GREEN against original :17871 same commit/host 2026-07-11; GREEN in prior-host full run and both rust full-suite runs

visual_baselines.note: Same visual profile as the prior host: the 6 screenshot-baselines assertions (maxDiffPixelRatio 0.05) all MATCH on this host's chromium-1208; editor-pane-loaded (NO tolerance) mismatches — baseline-strictness finding, not a functional regression.

baseline_semantics.reference: This exact 117-pass / 9-fail set (126 externally-targetable tests) is the T3 comparison target for the Rust port on host SurfaceBookPro9.
baseline_semantics.port_must: Keep all 117 currently-GREEN tests green on this host (a newly-RED test is a PORT_DEFECT) and keep the 6 matching visual baselines matching.
baseline_semantics.port_need_not: Pass the 9 tests RED against the original on this host (red==red is EQUIVALENT). Making any of them green is a candidate DELIBERATE_FIX (antagonist-adjudicated, ledgered).
baseline_semantics.why_red: All 9 reproduce against the PRISTINE original built from this worktree — 8 are the same EQUIVALENT-red set as the prior host; 1 (fresh-agent.spec.ts:98) is newly red on this host (see quarantined evidence).

delta_vs_prior_host.prior_profile: port/oracle/baselines/t3/summary.json (prior host): 118/126 externally-targetable pass after PORT-GAP closure; 8 EQUIVALENT-red fails (see EQUIVALENCE-REPORT.md §6).
delta_vs_prior_host.note: The 8 prior EQUIVALENT-red failures reproduce exactly on this host; fresh-agent.spec.ts:98 additionally fails here (pane picker shows a Freshclaude button where the spec expects none by default).

quarantined.description: Tests that FAIL against the pristine ORIGINAL on this host (findings, not port gates). Single run at workers=1 retries=0; not force-greened; baselines were not retaken.

items.title: loads the editor lazily and requests a new JS asset after the click
items.evidence: toHaveScreenshot(editor-pane-loaded.png) failed on [data-testid=editor-pane]; this assertion has NO maxDiffPixelRatio tolerance, unlike the 6 screenshot-baselines (0.05) which all MATCH.

items.title: normalizes remote legacy layout sync before exposing server pane snapshots
keeps fresh-agent settings and routes while legacy settings and routes are removed
items.evidence: getByText('Fresh agent') toBeVisible failed: element(s) not found.

items.title: pane picker hides fresh clients by default even when their CLIs are enabled
items.flaky_vs_hard: unclassified(single-run); NEW on this host (passed on prior host)
items.evidence: getByRole('button', {name:/^Freshclaude$/i}) toHaveCount(0) failed: Received 1 (locator resolved to 1 element for the full 10s timeout) at fresh-agent.spec.ts:117. The pane picker on this host shows the Freshclaude tile where the spec expects fresh clients hidden by default.

items.title: shows MRU tiles, sorted modal sources, and client-side filtering
items.evidence: openFreshAgentSettings: getByRole('group').filter(hasText 'Freshopencode').last() toBeVisible failed: element(s) not found (needs the opencode model catalog).

items.title: permission banner buttons are visible and functional on mobile
items.evidence: toContainText on getByRole('alert', {name:/permission request for bash/i}) failed.

items.title: reconnecting second viewer keeps page 1 PTY size stable and both pages keep shared output
items.evidence: page.waitForFunction: Timeout 20000ms exceeded (FRESHELL_E2E_TARGET_HOME was supplied, so serverInfo.homeDir was available).

items.title: enables multi-row tabs via settings toggle
items.evidence: getByRole('switch', {name:/multi-row tabs/i}) toBeVisible failed: element(s) not found after opening Settings.

items.title: freshclaude panes transition from waiting to blue running and back to idle
items.evidence: expectChromeBlue: pane icon toHaveClass(/text-blue-500/) failed (got 'lucide lucide-settings h-[18px] w-[18px] sm:h-3 sm:w-3') at pane-activity-indicator.spec.ts:24.

targetable_seam.purpose: External-target run of the identical e2e specs against a running server (here: the ORIGINAL on 127.0.0.1:17871) via FRESHELL_E2E_TARGET_URL.
targetable_seam.note: 126 = the 138-test full suite minus the 12 tests in the 6 server-owning spec files above.
